sentence: Hoje o tempo não está nada bom.
ipa: ˈɔʒɨ ˈo ˈtẽpu ˈnɐ̃w̃ eˈʃta ˈnadɐ ˈbõ
gloss_en: The weather is not good at all today.
notes: Inherited coda chiado [ʃ] in 'está' and reduction; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'não' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Estas cerejas custam cinco euros.
ipa: ˈeʃtɐs̺ sɨˈɾɛʒɐs̺ ˈkuʃtɐ̃ ˈsĩku ˈewɾus̺
gloss_en: These cherries cost five euros.
notes: Laminal [s] for ⟨c⟩ before e/i in 'cerejas'/'cinco' vs apical [s̺] word-final in 'estas'/'euros' — the four-sibilant contrast per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014.

sentence: A reunião é amanhã às sete da tarde.
ipa: ˈɐ ʁewniˈɐ̃w̃ ˈɛ ɐmɐˈɲã ˈas̺ ˈs̺ɛtɨ ˈdɐ ˈtaɾdɨ
gloss_en: The meeting is tomorrow at seven in the afternoon.
notes: Apical [s̺] in 'às'/'sete', palatal [ɲ] in 'amanhã' and strong [ʁ] in 'reunião' per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014.

sentence: O meu vizinho vive perto do souto.
ipa: ˈo ˈmew biˈziɲu ˈbibɨ ˈpɛɾtu ˈdu ˈs̺otu
gloss_en: My neighbour lives near the chestnut grove.
notes: Betacism ⟨v⟩ → [b] in 'vizinho'/'vive' with laminal [z] for ⟨z⟩; apical [s̺] and 'ou' in 'souto' per cintra1971 and pwl_trasosmontes.

sentence: Segue em frente e vira na terceira rua.
ipa: ˈs̺ɨɡɨ ˈẽ ˈfɾẽtɨ ˈe ˈbiɾɐ ˈnɐ tɨˈɾsejɾɐ ˈʁuɐ
gloss_en: Keep straight ahead and turn on the third street.
notes: Betacism [b] in 'vira'; 'ei' in 'terceira', apical [s̺] onset in 'segue' and strong [ʁ] in 'rua' per cintra1971 and pwl_trasosmontes.

sentence: O chouriço estava saboroso.
ipa: ˈo tʃoˈɾisu eˈʃtabɐ s̺ɐbuˈɾɔz̺u
gloss_en: The smoked sausage was tasty.
notes: Archaic ⟨ch⟩ affricate [tʃ] in 'chouriço' with laminal [s] for ⟨ç⟩; betacism [b] in 'estava' and 'ou' per cintra1971 and pwl_trasosmontes.

sentence: Onde é que fica a praça?
ipa: ˈõdɨ ˈɛ ˈkɨ ˈfikɐ ˈɐ ˈpɾasɐ
gloss_en: Where is the square?
notes: Laminal [s] for ⟨ç⟩ in 'praça'; reduced pretonics per cintra1971 and mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Quantas pessoas vêm ao jantar?
ipa: ˈkwɐ̃tɐs̺ pɨˈs̺ɔɐs̺ ˈbẽ ˈɐu ʒɐ̃ˈtaɾ
gloss_en: How many people are coming to dinner?
notes: Betacism [b] in 'vêm'; apical [s̺] in 'pessoas' and coda [ʃ] in 'quantas' per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014.

sentence: Se pudesse, comprava aquela vinha.
ipa: ˈs̺ɨ puˈdɛs̺ɨ kõˈpɾabɐ ɐˈkɛlɐ ˈbiɲɐ
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy that vineyard.
notes: Apical [s̺] in 'pudesse', betacism [b] and palatal [ɲ] in 'vinha' per cintra1971 and pwl_trasosmontes.

sentence: Comprámos vinho e castanhas na feira.
ipa: kõˈpɾamus̺ ˈbiɲu ˈe kɐˈʃtaɲɐs̺ ˈnɐ ˈfejɾɐ
gloss_en: We bought wine and chestnuts at the market.
notes: Betacism [b] and palatal [ɲ] in 'vinho'/'castanhas'; 'ei' in 'feira' per cintra1971 and pwl_trasosmontes.

sentence: Amanhã vamos de carro até Bragança.
ipa: ɐmɐˈɲã ˈbamus̺ ˈdɨ ˈkaʁu ɐˈtɛ bɾɐˈɡɐ̃sɐ
gloss_en: Tomorrow we go by car to Bragança.
notes: Betacism [b] in 'vamos'; strong [ʁ] in 'carro' and laminal [s] for ⟨ç⟩ in 'Bragança' per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014.

sentence: Telefona-me quando chegares a casa.
ipa: tɨlɨˈfɔnɐ ˈmɨ ˈkwɐ̃du tʃɨˈɡaɾɨs̺ ˈɐ ˈkaz̺ɐ
gloss_en: Call me when you get home.
notes: Archaic ⟨ch⟩ affricate [tʃ] in 'chegares' and intervocalic apical [z̺] in 'casa' per cintra1971 and pwl_trasosmontes.

sentence: Boa tarde, então como tem passado?
ipa: ˈbɔɐ ˈtaɾd ẽˈtɐ̃w̃ ˈkɔmu ˈtẽ pɐˈs̺adu
gloss_en: Good afternoon, so how have you been?
notes: Nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'então'; apical [s̺] in 'passado' per cintra1971 and mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Mais vale um pássaro na mão que dois a voar.
ipa: ˈmajs̺ ˈbalɨ ˈũ ˈpas̺ɐɾu ˈnɐ ˈmɐ̃w̃ ˈkɨ ˈdojs̺ ˈɐ buˈaɾ
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: Betacism [b] in 'vale'/'voar' and apical [s̺] in 'pássaro'; proverb per cintra1971 and mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O passo largo leva ao paço velho.
ipa: ˈo ˈpas̺u ˈlaɾɡu ˈlɛbɐ ˈɐu ˈpasu ˈbɛʎu
gloss_en: The wide step leads to the old palace.
notes: Four-sibilant minimal pair 'passo' [ˈpas̺u] apical vs 'paço' [ˈpasu] laminal; palatal [ʎ] in 'velho' and betacism [b] in 'leva' per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014.

sentence: A chuva caiu sobre o chão de barro.
ipa: ˈɐ ˈtʃubɐ kaˈju ˈs̺ɔbɾɨ ˈo ˈtʃɐ̃w̃ ˈdɨ ˈbaʁu
gloss_en: The rain fell on the earthen floor.
notes: Archaic ⟨ch⟩ affricate [tʃ] in 'chuva'/'chão' with betacism [b], nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] and strong [ʁ] in 'barro' per cintra1971 and pwl_trasosmontes.

sentence: O carro do primeiro rapaz ficou no outro lado.
ipa: ˈo ˈkaʁu ˈdu pɾiˈmejɾu ʁɐˈpaʃ fiˈko ˈnu ˈotɾu ˈladu
gloss_en: The first boy's car ended up on the other side.
notes: Strong [ʁ] in 'carro'; 'ei' in 'primeiro' and 'ou' in 'ficou'/'outro' per cintra1971 and mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O sol brilha sobre as colinas verdes.
ipa: ˈo ˈs̺ɔɫ ˈbɾiʎɐ ˈs̺ɔbɾɨ ˈɐs̺ kuˈlinɐs̺ ˈbɛɾdɨs̺
gloss_en: The sun shines over the green hills.
notes: Dark coda [ɫ] in 'sol', palatal [ʎ] in 'brilha', betacism [b] in 'verdes' and apical [s̺] finals per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014.

sentence: A casa nova fica perto da igreja.
ipa: ˈɐ ˈkaz̺ɐ ˈnɔbɐ ˈfikɐ ˈpɛɾtu ˈdɐ iˈɡɾɛʒɐ
gloss_en: The new house is near the church.
notes: Intervocalic apical [z̺] in 'casa', betacism [b] in 'nova' and open-mid [ɛ ɔ] per cintra1971 and pwl_trasosmontes.

sentence: Em dezembro comemos castanhas e cerejas.
ipa: ˈẽ dɨˈzẽbɾu kuˈmɛmus̺ kɐˈʃtaɲɐs̺ ˈe sɨˈɾɛʒɐs̺
gloss_en: In December we eat chestnuts and cherries.
notes: Laminal [z] for ⟨z⟩ in 'dezembro' and [s] for ⟨c⟩ in 'cerejas' vs apical [s̺] finals; palatal [ɲ] in 'castanhas' — four-sibilant contrast per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014.
